questions: You discovered that the 3x cost difference between single-agent and multi-agent versions only mattered at certain scales—what made you decide to build the expensive version anyway if you're only using it 10 times a week?
When you first tried passing everything as parameters in the Conversation Manager, how did you recognize it was becoming unmaintainable before it actually broke in production?
The tool-enabled agent gave you a completely different (and correct) answer than the one without tools—did this change how you think about whether agents are actually 'reasoning' or just executing?
